project_title: Clinic Appointment and Triage Support System

functional_requirements.statement: Patients can request an appointment by providing necessary details.
acceptance_criteria: Patients can submit name, NHS number, date of birth, preferred date/time, visit reason.
System prevents duplicate appointment requests for the same patient within the same day.

functional_requirements.statement: Reception staff can view and manage appointment requests.
acceptance_criteria: Receptionists can view all appointment requests.
Receptionists can confirm or reschedule an appointment.
Receptionists can assign clinicians to appointments.

functional_requirements.statement: Clinicians can manage triage notes and mark urgency.
acceptance_criteria: Clinicians can mark cases as urgent or routine.
Clinicians can update appointment outcome as Seen, Follow-up Required, Cancelled, Did Not Attend.

functional_requirements.statement: Patients receive notifications regarding appointment status.
acceptance_criteria: Notifications sent when an appointment is confirmed, rescheduled, or cancelled.

functional_requirements.statement: The system maintains an audit log of key actions.
acceptance_criteria: Audit log includes request created, appointment confirmed/rescheduled/cancelled, clinician assigned, triage notes added, urgency changed, outcome updated.
Users authenticate as Patients, Receptionists, or Clinicians.
Access to patient information is restricted by role.

functional_requirements.statement: System validates required fields and NHS numbers.
acceptance_criteria: Empty or invalid NHS numbers are rejected during appointment requests.

non_functional_requirements.statement: System responds within 2 seconds for viewing today’s appointment list under normal clinic load.

assumptions: Assume normal clinic load is defined by usual patient volume.
System will connect to a secure, existing patient database for validation.
clarification_questions: What is the normal clinic load in terms of patient volume per day?
What are the specific security measures required for data storage?

glossary.definition: A unique identifier used for patients within the UK National Health Service.
The process by which the urgency of patient treatment is determined.
